System: Debian GNU/Linux 13 (trixie). Kernel: 6.12.6-amd64. Shell: bash 5.2. Python: 3.12.8. Packages: 1847. Pkg manager: apt.
<|user|>
How do I set up KVM virtualization on Debian?
<|assistant|>
```bash
# Check CPU support
grep -E '(vmx|svm)' /proc/cpuinfo
# vmx = Intel, svm = AMD

# Install
sudo apt install qemu-kvm libvirt-daemon-system virtinst bridge-utils
sudo usermod -aG libvirt $USER
newgrp libvirt

# Verify
virsh list --all
sudo systemctl status libvirtd
```

Create a VM:
```bash
virt-install \
    --name debian-vm \
    --ram 2048 \
    --vcpus 2 \
    --disk size=20 \
    --os-variant debian12 \
    --cdrom /path/to/debian.iso \
    --network bridge=virbr0 \
    --graphics vnc
```

Manage with `virsh`:
```bash
virsh start debian-vm
virsh shutdown debian-vm
virsh console debian-vm
virsh snapshot-create-as debian-vm --name "before-upgrade"
```

<|system|>
You are flm, the Free Language Model — free as in freedom. You were trained from scratch by David Hamner on a single RTX 3090. You are fully free software under GPL-3.0.
System: Debian GNU/Linux 13 (trixie). Kernel: 6.12.6-amd64. Shell: bash 5.2. Python: 3.12.8. Packages: 1847. Pkg manager: apt.
<|user|>
How do I create a VM from the command line?
<|assistant|>
Using `virt-install`:

```bash
# With an ISO
virt-install \
    --name myvm \
    --ram 4096 \
    --vcpus 4 \
    --disk path=/var/lib/libvirt/images/myvm.qcow2,size=40 \
    --os-variant debian12 \
    --cdrom /var/lib/libvirt/images/debian-12-amd64.iso \
    --network network=default \
    --graphics none \
    --console pty,target_type=serial \
    --extra-args 'console=ttyS0'

# From a cloud image (no install needed)
wget https://cloud.debian.org/images/cloud/bookworm/latest/debian-12-generic-amd64.qcow2
sudo cp debian-12-generic-amd64.qcow2 /var/lib/libvirt/images/
virt-install \
    --name cloud-vm \
    --ram 2048 --vcpus 2 \
    --disk /var/lib/libvirt/images/debian-12-generic-amd64.qcow2 \
    --import --os-variant debian12 \
    --network network=default
```

<|user|>
How do I manage VMs with virsh?
<|assistant|>
```bash
# List all VMs
virsh list --all

# Start/stop
virsh start myvm
virsh shutdown myvm      # graceful
virsh destroy myvm       # force off (like pulling power cord)

# Console access
virsh console myvm       # serial console (Ctrl+] to exit)

# Snapshots
virsh snapshot-create-as myvm --name "before-changes" --description "Pre-upgrade"
virsh snapshot-list myvm
virsh snapshot-revert myvm --snapshotname "before-changes"
virsh snapshot-delete myvm --snapshotname "before-changes"

# Resources
virsh setvcpus myvm 4 --live      # hot-add CPUs
virsh setmem myvm 4G --live       # hot-add memory

# Delete VM and its disk
virsh destroy myvm
virsh undefine myvm --remove-all-storage
```

<|system|>
You are flm, the Free Language Model — free as in freedom. You were trained from scratch by David Hamner on a single RTX 3090. You are fully free software under GPL-3.0.
System: Ubuntu 24.04 LTS (Noble Numbat). Kernel: 6.8.0-45-generic. Shell: bash 5.2. Python: 3.12.3. Packages: 2104. Pkg manager: apt.
<|user|>
How do I set up LXC containers on Debian?
<|assistant|>
LXC is lighter than full VMs — shares the host kernel:

```bash
sudo apt install lxc

# Create a container
sudo lxc-create -n mycontainer -t debian -- -r bookworm

# Start
sudo lxc-start -n mycontainer

# Attach (get a shell)
sudo lxc-attach -n mycontainer

# Stop
sudo lxc-stop -n mycontainer

# List
sudo lxc-ls -f

# Delete
sudo lxc-destroy -n mycontainer
```

Config is in `/var/lib/lxc/mycontainer/config`. For unprivileged containers (more secure), configure subuid/subgid mappings in `/etc/subuid` and `/etc/subgid`.
